title: Gemini’s guided learning: results from a randomized controlled trial in Sierra Leone
gist: A pre-registered RCT of Gemini's Guided Learning across 1,763 junior-secondary math students in Sierra Leone produced a +0.258 SD score gain (roughly 1.2–1.7 years of learning in 8 weeks, up to 1.8–2.5 years at higher usage), with 69% of students hitting usage targets versus the ~5% typical for voluntary edtech. DeepMind frames the product as augmenting teachers via Socratic scaffolding rather than answer-giving, and is scaling to further global RCTs.

insights.claim: Students using Gemini Guided Learning gained +0.258 standard deviations in math scores versus control over an 8-week RCT, equivalent to roughly 1.2–1.7 years of typical learning progress.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: A large, pre-registered effect size is the kind of hard evidence that unlocks government/NGO edtech budgets and validates LLM tutoring as a real product category.
insights.quote: Students using Guided Learning saw a gain of +0.258 standard deviations in their math scores compared to the control group.

insights.claim: Students whose teachers used Gemini in about half their lessons (targeting 12 hours over the trial) saw the largest gains, roughly 1.8–2.5 years of progress in eight weeks.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: A dose-response relationship strengthens the causal case and gives deployers a concrete usage target to maximize ROI.

insights.claim: 69% of students met or exceeded usage targets, far above the ~5% engagement typical for voluntary educational technology (the 'Five Percent Problem').
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: Sustained engagement is the historic killer of edtech ROI; a 14x improvement over the norm is the metric that separates a deployable product from a pilot.
insights.quote: 69% of students met or exceeded usage targets, far surpassing the five percent typical for voluntary educational technology

insights.claim: Guided Learning is tuned to prioritize building understanding over giving direct answers; across 113,000+ logged interactions, Gemini posed scaffolding questions in 76% of messages and gave direct solutions in only 2%.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: Pedagogical tuning (LearnLM) is the moat versus generic chatbots and the answer to the 'AI as cheating shortcut' objection that gates institutional adoption.
insights.quote: Gemini responded by posing scaffolding questions in 76% of its messages, providing direct solutions in only 2% of cases.
insights.locator: Beyond the answer engine: protecting critical thinking

insights.claim: Student behavior shifted over the trial: skill-building queries rose from 68% in week one to 90% by the final week, while solution-seeking questions fell from 25% to 10%.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: Behavioral change over time, not just test scores, is the evidence that the Socratic design durably alters how students use AI.
insights.quote: skill-building queries rose to 90% by the final week — up from 68% in the first week — while solution-seeking questions dropped from 25% to 10%

insights.claim: DeepMind positions AI as augmenting teachers rather than replacing them, with educators designing lessons and facilitating, and many teachers shifting from 'lecturers' to 'facilitators'.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: The teacher-centered framing is deliberate go-to-market: it lowers union/political resistance and reframes AI as a complement, easing public-sector procurement.
insights.quote: AI can be a powerful pedagogical partner — not by replacing teachers, but by augmenting their reach.

insights.claim: The benefit was uneven: students who entered with stronger math skills benefited most, highlighting an achievement-gap risk that the tool does not yet close.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: If AI tutoring widens rather than narrows gaps, it complicates the equity case funders and governments rely on to justify deployment.
insights.quote: those who entered the trial with stronger math skills benefited most

insights.claim: DeepMind is running additional pre-registered RCTs globally with Fab AI, releasing an RCT playbook and teacher training guide, and supporting the Global AI for Learning Alliance (GAILA) to build a cross-country evidence base.
insights.speaker: Zoubin Ghahramani
insights.so_what: A coordinated multi-country evidence push signals Google's intent to make AI education a durable, policy-backed market rather than a one-off PR pilot.
insights.locator: Measuring the impact / To further understand the impact
